system_prompt: You are a CFD expert. Based on the user requirement and the retrieved similar cases, produce a concise usage guidance. If no suitable case is available, set match_level to 'none' and advise not to rely on similar case templates.
user_prompt:
User requirement:
Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 310 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.

Case info:
case name: BernardCells
case domain: heatTransfer
case category: RAS
case solver: buoyantFoam

Selected similar case:
BernardCells | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.0460662841796875

Top candidates:
- BernardCells | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.0460662841796875
- buoyantCavity | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.38416588306427
- hotRadiationRoom | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.4204709827899933
- circuitBoardCooling | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.42444920539855957
- hotRoom | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.4272984266281128

Return JSON with keys: match_level (high/medium/low/none), use_scope, advice.
response: match_level='high' use_scope='Use the BernardCells tutorial case as the primary template for all configuration files including blockMeshDict, controlDict, thermophysicalProperties, turbulenceProperties, and boundary conditions; adjust domain dimensions to 9x1x2 m, endTime to 1000 s, deltaT to 1 s, writeInterval to 50, and wall temperatures to 310 K and 300 K as specified.' advice="Directly adopt the BernardCells tutorial case structure and files as the baseline template since it is an exact match in solver, physics, and case type, then modify the mesh dimensions to 9 m × 1 m × 2 m, time controls (endTime=1000, deltaT=1, writeInterval=50), and thermal boundary conditions (hot wall=310 K, cold wall=300 K) to meet the user's specific requirements."
